opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The OS-resident approach avoids a network hop, which is a significant latency win for real-time agent interaction. However, sandboxing and permission management within the OS become critical; you’ll quickly encounter kernel-level friction when agents need to interact with hardware or protected system resources, demanding a careful design of privilege delegation. It’s a fascinating tradeoff between performance and security.
actions.reasoning: My experience building AI agents and routing architectures gives me a specific perspective on the technical challenges presented by an OS-resident agent.
